race.name: Marcialonga
race.tagline: Seventy kilometers beneath the Dolomite spires — Italy's frozen masterpiece where alpine grandeur meets the longest Worldloppet classic.

vitals.registration: Lottery system; registration opens in summer
vitals.aid_stations: 6 feed stations with Italian pastries, warm drinks, and energy food

climate.description: January race date means consistent cold. The valley location between Dolomite peaks provides some wind shelter but can trap cold air. Temperatures typically -10°C to -2°C. Snow is generally reliable at altitude but January warm spells can soften lower sections.
challenges: Valley fog can reduce visibility in early kilometers
Snow quality varies between shaded and sun-exposed valley sections
Late January thaw risk at lower elevations

course.primary: Point-to-point through the Val di Fiemme and Val di Fassa, following the Avisio River valley beneath the Dolomites. Rolling terrain with moderate climbs, passing through multiple villages. The course is predominantly valley-floor skiing with stunning mountain scenery above.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through alpine valley terrain
course.grooming: World-class Worldloppet standard, expertly maintained valley tracks
features: Dolomite panorama throughout the entire course
Italian feed stations with local pastries and espresso
Valley skiing along the Avisio River
Finish in Cavalese — also a FIS World Cup venue

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Maximum scenery score anchored by Dolomite views. The Italian hospitality and alpine setting create an atmosphere unmatched in long-distance skiing. Only snow reliability prevents a perfect score.

history.summary: Founded in 1971 as a 70km classic race through the Val di Fiemme and Val di Fassa in the Italian Dolomites. It quickly grew into one of the most prestigious events on the Worldloppet circuit. The race celebrates the Ladin culture of the valleys and has become Italy's defining cross-country ski marathon. The Val di Fiemme has also hosted multiple FIS Nordic World Ski Championships.
notable_facts: Val di Fiemme has hosted FIS Nordic World Ski Championships in 1991, 2003, and 2013
The Ladin-speaking valleys are a unique cultural enclave within Italy
Italian feed stations famously serve strudel, hot chocolate, and espresso
One of the original founding members of the Worldloppet series
Part of both Worldloppet and Ski Classics Grand Classic series
The race name means 'Long March' in Italian

videos.title: Marcialonga 2019 1080p
videos.curation_reason: Recent race footage in HD quality showing the 70km Nordic course from skier perspective.

videos.title: Marcialonga, l'impresa dei «bisonti» sotto la neve
videos.description: Neve fitta, fatica estrema e imprese da ricordare: la Marcialonga 2026 entra nella leggenda. La 53ª edizione della granfondo di Fiemme e Fassa si è corsa in condizioni epiche, sotto una nevicata incessante che ha trasformato i 70 chilometri da Moena a Cavalese in una sfida quasi eroica. A domare il meteo e il percorso sono stati i norvegesi Kasper Stadaas ed Emilie Fleten, trionfatori di una giornata destinata a restare nella storia.

videos.title: Marcialonga 2023 Race report vlogg Magnus
videos.description:
Race report från Marcialonga 2023. En film inifrån loppet, filmat med GoPro, ur åkarens (mitt) perspektiv - en vlogg över loppet. 
Marcialonga ingår i Ski Classics långloppscup i längdskidåkning och räknas som Italiens Vasalopp. Det går vid foten av Dolomiterna i en dalgång. Som åkare passerar man många små pitoreska byar på vägen mellan Moena (starten) och Cavalese (målet). 

Innehåll:
00:00 Intro innan start
01:07 Start till Canazei
04:23 Canazei till Predazzo
10:20 Predazzo till Molina
13:30 Molina till Cascata
15:00 Cascata till målet
17:00 Målgång och after-ski
videos.curation_reason: Recent first-person race vlog with GoPro footage from skier's perspective covering the full 70km course from Moena to Cavalese.

videos.title: 47th Marcialonga (26.01.2020)
videos.description:
Marcialonga is called "The Queen of Italian Nordic ski Granfondo". For two consecutive years (2018-2019) it has won the prize “Best Event of the year” proposed by Visma Ski Classic.
Marcialonga takes place in Val di Fiemme and Val di Fassa, mountain areas just under the majestic Dolomites, which make Trentino region one of the most attractive places in Europe to spend an holiday.
Marcialonga represents a Story of sport and tradition that has survived for over 50 years involving everybody from 0 to 100 with different events…

The course offers two different lengths: the classic race of 70 km from Moena to Cavalese, or the Light version with the finish line in Predazzo after 45 km. The slope leads skiers through the villages of the valleys, discovering unique and suggestive views and the support of many spectators cheering for more than 7200 participants.

videos.title: hillrunner68@ Marcialonga 2015
videos.description: crosscountry classic in Val di Fiemme, Predazzo, Cavalese,#xc-ski.de

videos.title: Marcialonga 2017
videos.description:
(IT) La Marcialonga, conosciuta anche come Marcialonga di Fiemme e Fassa o Marcialonga skiing, è la più importante e famosa competizione granfondo di sci di fondo in Italia e si disputa sulle nevi delle Valli di Fiemme e Fassa (Trento), l'ultima domenica di gennaio.

Fonte: Wikipedia
----------
(EN) Marcialonga is a cross-country ski race which is arranged for the last Sunday in January in Trentino in Italy. It was first held in 1971 and has been a part of Worldloppet as long as Worldloppet has been around.[1] In 2007, it was run for the 34th time.
The run begins in Moena, where the skiers first go further up Fassa Valley and along Canazei, passing Moena on its way alongside Cavalese in the Fiemme Valley.
Marcialonga runs in classic style, and is 70 km long, but in 2007, 2015 and 2017, it was shortened to 57 km due to snow problems. In 1975, 1989 and 1990 it was cancelled due to the total lack of snow on the track. A short race, Marcialonga light is 45 km, and goes from Moena to Predazzo.

Source: Wikipedia
----------
(DE) Der Marcialonga oder Marcialonga di fiemme e fassa ist ein Skilanglaufmarathon und einer von sechzehn Läufen der Worldloppet-Serie. Der Marcialonga war wiederholt Bestandteil des Skilanglauf-Marathon-Cups und gehört seit 2011 zur Wettlaufserie von Ski Classics. Er findet im Fleimstal (Val di Fiemme) und im Fassatal (Val di Fassa) in den Dolomiten in Italien statt.

Quelle: Wikipedia
----------
(NO) Marcialonga er et turrenn på ski som arrangeres hver siste søndag i januar i provinsen Trento i Italia. I 2017 ble rennet arrangert for 44. gang. Rennet inngår i FIS Worldloppet Cup og Ski Classics.
Rennet begynner i Moena, hvor løperne først går videre opp i Fassadalen og snur ved Cianacei og passerer Moena på sin vei mot målgang i Cavalese i Val di Fiemme. Jerry Ahrlin har løyperekorden fra 2011 med 2.49.10.
Marcialonga foregår i klassisk stil og er 70 km langt, men i 2003, 2007, 2015 og 2017 måtte traseen forkortes til 57 km på grunn av snømangel. En forkortet løype Marcialonga light på 45 km går fra Moena til Predazzo.

Kilde: Wikipedia
----------

videos.title: Marcialonga 2021 Me standing in front of La Cascata (final 3 km uphill) loosing composure
videos.description:
After 67 km of skiing you stand in front of the final 3 km UPHILL to Cavalese... Madness? THIS IS MARCIALONGA! 

In 2021 I skied my first Marcialonga and it was very tough but great. I definitely will do it again. Even tough I almost lost my composure. 

One hint from my side: If you feel the need to pee during a ski marathon, just go! Nobody cares if you will finish 45 seconds later. 

Registration Marcialonga: 
https://www.marcialonga.it/marcialonga_ski/

Worldloppet Passport: 
https://www.worldloppet.com/prodotto/worldloppet-passport/

#marcialonga #scidifondo #langrenn #crosscountryskiing #xcskiing

videos.title: MARCIALONGA  2025 SCI FONDO | FIEMME E FASSA | TRENTINO
videos.description:
La 52.a Marcialonga è degli stoici Emilie Fleten e Andreas Nygaard, più forti della pioggia
Per Fleten la 2.a vittoria consecutiva in una gara fatta di cadute, recuperi e grande forma
Per Nygaard il successo dopo 10 anni di tentativi conquistato con il lavoro di squadra e il guizzo finale
15.a Michaela Patscheider, 32° Lorenzo Busin e continua la festa per quasi 7000 bisonti.
“Marcialonga bagnata, Marcialonga fortunata” devono aver pensato in molti questa mattina alla partenza da Moena, quando ad accogliere bisonti e professionisti di Ski Classics c’era un vero tempo da lupi. Ma i migliori atleti al mondo delle lunghe distanze si sono dimostrati ancora una volta più forti di tutto, e al loro seguito migliaia di bisonti. I vincitori della 52.a edizione della Marcialonga di Fiemme e Fassa sono i norvegesi Emilie Fleten e Andreas Nygaard che conquistano la granfondo più amata al mondo nel segno della resilienza con un forcing di spessore sul Mur de la Stria (muro della strega).

#marcialonga @ValdiFassaSkiWorldCup #valdifassa #valdifiemme #scifondo @SciClubMarcialonga

videos.title: Marcialonga 2017 & Gita in Val di Fiemme (Trentino, Italia)
videos.description:
29.01.2017 Visma Ski Classics
Tra la Val di Fassa e la Val di Fiemme si svolge una delle gare più importanti dello sci da fondo.
Ho avuto la possibilità di vedere questa gara da due luoghi: lo stadio del fondo di Tesero e dal centro di Cavalese centro della Magnifica Comunità

Trentino, Italy. Ski marathon 
Between the Val di Fassa and Val di Fiemme it takes place one of the most important races for cross country skiing.

Trentino, Italia. Mellom Val di Fassa og Val di Fiemme det foregår en av de viktigste løp for langrenn.

Trentino, Italien. Zwischen dem Val di Fassa und Val di Fiemme, einem der wichtigsten Rennen für den Langlauf.

29.01.2017 Висма Ski Classics
Между Валь-ди-Фасса и Валь-ди-Фьемме, это имеет место одно из самых важных гонок для катания на беговых лыжах.
У меня была возможность увидеть эту гонку два места: стадион Фонда в Tesero и центр Cavalese центра Magnificent сообщества

videos.title: Atomic / Nordic diaries - Marcialonga
videos.description:
Atomic athletes Simen Østensen, Jørgen Aukland and Anders Aukland dominated this year's 70 km Marcialonga (Italy) finishing first, third and fifth. In this episode of Atomic's "Nordic Diaries", we give you a closer insight into what it takes to win one of the world's most prestigious XC long distance races. From hard training to perfect equipment, from camaraderie to collective effort. Click play to see how these guys think and feel before and after the race.

Video & Editing: Outdoor Live - www.outdoor-live.com

Music:
"Dust on this Broken Music Box"  by So I'm an Islander
"Wild guide" by Austin Leonard Jones
"The Temperature of the Air on the Bow of the Kaleetan" by Chris Zabriskie

videos.curation_reason: Professional race coverage with elite athlete insights, course breakdown, and detailed analysis of the three distinct race sections.

quotes.text: You can divide Marcialonga into three parts: the first 15K where you go from the start to Canazei, it's gradual uphill all the way, then you turn around and it's just down the valley high speed, then you turn again and it's 5K to the finish and it's more uphill with the last three K's pretty steep.

Something happens with the body when it passes two and a half hours and that's when you really see who's the best. You have to be really focused to get the feeding during the race and to drink energy drink.
